sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: στείχοιμʼ ἄν , εἴ τις ἡγεμὼν γίγνοιθʼ ὁδοῦ .
sections.translation: I would go , if some one would show me the way .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐγὼ πέμποιμʼ ἂν οὐκ ἀκουσίως .
sections.translation: Yes , I can escort you myself , not against my will .

sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ Πατρῷε , καὶ Τροπαῖʼ ἐχθρῶν γενοῦ —
sections.translation: O Zeus , god of my fathers , be also the vanquisher of my enemies —

sections.label: Section 672
sections.original: οἴκτιρέ θʼ ἡμᾶς · οἰκτρὰ γὰρ πεπόνθαμεν —
sections.translation: And have pity on us ; for we have suffered pitiably —

sections.label: Section 673
sections.original: οἴκτιρε δῆτα σούς γε φύντας ἐκγόνους .
sections.translation: Yes , indeed , have pity on your own descendants .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: Ἥρα τε , βωμῶν ἣ Μυκηναίων κρατεῖς —
sections.translation: And Hera , you who rule Mycenae ’ s altars —

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: νίκην δὸς ἡμῖν , εἰ δίκαιʼ αἰτούμεθα .
sections.translation: Give us victory , if we are asking for what is right .

sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: δὸς δῆτα πατρὸς τοῖσδε τιμωρὸν δίκην .
sections.translation: Yes , indeed , give them the right of vengeance for their father .

sections.label: Section 677
sections.original: σύ τʼ , ὦ κάτω γῆς ἀνοσίως οἰκῶν πάτερ —
sections.translation: You too , father , living below the earth through an unholy deed —

sections.label: Section 678
sections.original: καὶ Γαῖʼ ἄνασσα , χεῖρας ᾗ δίδωμʼ ἐμὰς —
sections.translation: And Lady Earth , to whom I give my hands —

sections.label: Section 679
sections.original: ἄμυνʼ ἄμυνε τοῖσδε φιλτάτοις τέκνοις .
sections.translation: Defend , defend these , your dearest children .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: νῦν πάντα νεκρὸν ἐλθὲ σύμμαχον λαβών .
sections.translation: Now come and bring with you all the dead as allies .

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: οἵπερ γε σὺν σοὶ Φρύγας ἀνήλωσαν δορὶ —
sections.translation: Those who destroyed the Trojans in war with you —

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: χὥσοι στυγοῦσιν ἀνοσίους μιάστορας .
sections.translation: Do you hear me , you who suffered dreadful things from my mother ?

sections.label: Section 682
sections.original: ἤκουσας , ὦ δείνʼ ἐξ ἐμῆς μητρὸς παθών ;
sections.translation: And all who hate the unholy and polluted .

sections.label: Section 684
sections.original: πάντʼ , οἶδʼ , ἀκούει τάδε πατήρ · στείχειν δʼ ἀκμή .
sections.translation: Your father hears everything , I know ; but it is time to be on our way .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: καί σοι προφωνῶ πρὸς τάδʼ Αἴγισθον θανεῖν ·
sections.translation: And I tell you therefore that Aegisthus is to die ; if you fall dead in the struggle , I am also dead , do not count me as alive ; for I will strike my heart with a two-edged sword . I will go indoors and make things ready there .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: ὡς ἢν μὲν ἔλθῃ πύστις εὐτυχὴς σέθεν ,
sections.translation: So that if a good report comes from you , the whole house will cry aloud in triumph ; but , if you die , it will be the opposite of that . These are my words to you .

sections.label: Section 693
sections.original: πάντʼ οἶδα .
sections.translation: I know it all .

sections.label: Section 693b
sections.original: πρὸς τάδʼ ἄνδρα γίγνεσθαί σε χρή .
sections.translation: Therefore you must be a man . Exeunt Orestes , Pylades , and Old Man . And you , women , please take care to give

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: κραυγὴν ἀγῶνος τοῦδε · φρουρήσω δʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: a shout in signal of this contest . I will keep a sword ready , holding it in my hand , for I will not ever , if defeated , submit to my enemies the right to insult my body . Exit Electra .

sections.label: Section 1.699
sections.original: ἀταλᾶς ὑπὸ ματρὸς ἄρνʼ
sections.translation: The story remains in old legends

sections.label: Section 1.700
sections.original: ὀρέων ποτὲ κληδὼν ἐν
sections.translation: that Pan , the keeper of wild beasts , breathing sweet-voiced music on his well-joined pipes , once brought from its tender mother on Argive hills

sections.label: Section 1.705
sections.original: χρυσέαν καλλιπλόκαμον
sections.translation: a lamb with beautiful golden fleece . A herald stood on the stone platform and cried aloud , To assembly , Mycenaeans , go to assembly

sections.label: Section 1.710
sections.original: ὀψόμενοι τυράννων
sections.translation: to see the omens given to our blessed rulers . . . . and they honored the house of Atreus .

sections.label: Section 1.713
sections.original: θυμέλαι δʼ ἐπίτναντο χρυ-
sections.translation: The altars of beaten gold were set out ; and through the town the

sections.label: Section 1.715
sections.original: σελαγεῖτο δʼ ἀνʼ ἄστυ πῦρ
sections.translation: altar fires of the Argives blazed ; the flute , handmaid of the Muse ’ s song , sounded its note sweetly , and lovely songs of the golden lamb swelled forth , saying that Thyestes had the luck ; for he

sections.label: Section 1.720
sections.original: Θυέστου · κρυφίαις γὰρ εὐ-
sections.translation: persuaded Atreus ’ own wife to secret love , and carried off to his house the portent ; coming before the assembly he declared that he had in his

sections.label: Section 1.725
sections.original: λον κατὰ δῶμα ποίμναν .
sections.translation: house the horned sheep with fleece of gold .

sections.label: Section 2.726
sections.original: τότε δὴ τότε φαεν-
sections.translation: Then , it was then that Zeus changed the radiant paths of the stars , and the light of the sun , and the

sections.label: Section 2.730
sections.original: λευκόν τε πρόσωπον ἀ-
sections.translation: bright face of dawn ; and the sun drove across the western back of the sky with hot flame from heaven ’ s fires , while the rain-clouds went northward and Ammon ’ s

sections.label: Section 2.735
sections.original: δραι φθίνουσʼ ἀπειρόδροσοι ,
sections.translation: lands grew parched and faint , not knowing moisture , robbed of heaven ’ s fairest showers of rain .

sections.label: Section 2.737
sections.original: λέγεται , τὰν δὲ πί-
sections.translation: It is said , but I have small belief in it , that the sun turned round his glowing

sections.label: Section 2.740
sections.original: χρυσωπὸν ἕδραν ἀλλά-
sections.translation: throne of gold , changing it to the misfortune of mankind , for the punishment of mortals . But tales that frighten men are profitable for service to the gods ; of whom you had no thought , when you

sections.label: Section 2.745
sections.original: αν . ὧν οὐ μνασθεῖσα πόσιν
sections.translation: killed your husband , you who are the relative of famous brothers .

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: ἔα ἔα ·
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! My friends , did you hear a noise — or did an empty notion come to me ?— like the underground rumbling from Zeus ? Look , the breeze rises , bringing with it a sign .

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: δέσποινʼ , ἄμειψον δώματʼ , Ἠλέκτρα , τάδε .
sections.translation: Mistress , Electra , leave the house !

sections.label: Section 751
sections.original: φίλαι , τί χρῆμα ; πῶς ἀγῶνος ἥκομεν ;
sections.translation: My friends , what is it ? How do we stand in the contest ?

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα πλὴν ἕν · φόνιον οἰμωγὴν κλύω .
sections.translation: I only know this ; I hear a wailing that means bloodshed .

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: ἤκουσα κἀγώ , τηλόθεν μέν , ἀλλʼ ὅμως .
sections.translation: I heard it also , far off , but still heard .

sections.label: Section 754
sections.original: μακρὰν γὰρ ἕρπει γῆρυς , ἐμφανής γε μήν .
sections.translation: Yes , the sound is coming a long way , but it is clear .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: Ἀργεῖος ὁ στεναγμός · ἦ φίλων ἐμῶν ;
sections.translation: The groan was of an Argive ; was it from my friends ?

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα · πᾶν γὰρ μείγνυται μέλος βοῆς .
sections.translation: I don ’ t know ; for the whole tune of the shout is confused .

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: σφαγὴν ἀυτεῖς τήνδε μοι · τί μέλλομεν ;
sections.translation: You are calling out to me my death ; why do I delay ?

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: ἔπισχε , τρανῶς ὡς μάθῃς τύχας σέθεν .
sections.translation: Hold back , to learn your fortune clearly .

sections.label: Section 759
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι · νικώμεσθα · ποῦ γὰρ ἄγγελοι ;
sections.translation: No , no ; we are vanquished ; where are the messengers ?

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ἥξουσιν · οὔτοι βασιλέα φαῦλον κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: They will come ; it is no trivial matter to kill a king .

sections.label: Section 761
sections.original: ὦ καλλίνικοι παρθένοι Μυκηνίδες ,
sections.translation: O victorious maidens of Mycenae , I report to all his friends that Orestes has conquered , and Aegisthus , the murderer of Agamemnon , lies on the ground ; but we must offer prayers to the gods .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: τίς δʼ εἶ σύ ; πῶς μοι πιστὰ σημαίνεις τάδε ;
sections.translation: Who are you ? How trustworthy is your announcement ?

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθʼ ἀδελφοῦ μʼ εἰσορῶσα πρόσπολον ;
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know your brother ’ s servant when you look at me ?

sections.label: Section 767
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ἔκ τοι δείματος δυσγνωσίαν
sections.translation: O best of friends ! I could not recognize your face out of fear ; but now I know you well . What are you saying ? Is my father ’ s hated murderer dead ?

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: τέθνηκε · δίς σοι ταὔθʼ , ἃ γοῦν βούλῃ , λέγω .
sections.translation: He is dead ; I am telling you twice what you certainly want to hear .

sections.label: Section 771
sections.original: ὦ θεοί , Δίκη τε πάνθʼ ὁρῶσʼ , ἦλθές ποτε .
sections.translation: O gods , and all-seeing justice , at last you have come . In what way and by what form of death did he kill Thyestes ’ son ? I would like to learn .

sections.label: Section 774
sections.original: ἐπεὶ μελάθρων τῶνδʼ ἀπήραμεν πόδα ,
sections.translation: After we had left this house ,

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἐσβάντες ᾖμεν δίκροτον εἰς ἁμαξιτὸν
sections.translation: we stepped onto the broad highway and went to the place where the famous King of Mycenae was . He turned out to be walking in a well-watered garden , plucking a wreath of tender myrtle-sprays for his head ; when he saw us , he called out , Welcome , strangers ! Who are you

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: πόθεν πορεύεσθʼ ; ἔστε τʼ ἐκ ποίας χθονός ;
sections.translation: and from where do you come ? from what country ? Orestes said , We are Thessalians , going to the Alpheus river to sacrifice to Olympian Zeus . When Aegisthus heard that , he said , You must be my guests for the feast

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: θοίνης γενέσθαι · τυγχάνω δὲ βουθυτῶν
sections.translation: with us now , for I happen to be sacrificing an ox to the Nymphs ; and if you get out of bed at dawn , it will make no difference to you . But let us go within — while he was addressing us , he took us by the hand and led us off the road — you must not refuse .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δʼ ἐν οἴκοις ἦμεν , ἐννέπει τάδε ·
sections.translation: And when we were in the house , he gave the command : Let someone bring water immediately for my guests , so that they may stand around the altar near the basin . But Orestes said : Just now we purified ourselves in clean water from the river ’ s streams .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: εἰ δὲ ξένους ἀστοῖσι συνθύειν χρεών ,
sections.translation: So if strangers must join in the sacrifice with citizens , Aegisthus , we are ready and will not refuse , lord . So they ended their public conversation . The slaves who formed the master ’ s bodyguard laid aside their spears , and all applied their hands to the work .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: οἳ μὲν σφαγεῖον ἔφερον , οἳ δʼ ᾖρον κανᾶ ,
sections.translation: Some brought the bowl to catch the blood , others took up baskets , while others kindled fire and set cauldrons around the hearth , and the whole roof rang . Then your mother ’ s bed-fellow took barley for sprinkling , and cast it upon the altar with these words ,
